title: podman remote fails with connection error
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot add a podman remote

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On a Mac brew install podman; receive latest version, 2.1.1

2. On a CentOS Stream box, set up the podman socket, and sshd per instructions.  Note: version 2.0.5 seems to be the latest.  SSH is working as expected

3.  On the Mac run `podman system connection add srv 192.168.0.227`

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: failed to connect to  "ssh://bexelbie@192.168.0.227:22": failed to parse 'podman info' results: json: cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field Version.version.APIVersion of type string

**Describe the results you expected:**

A connection created

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens consistently.  Downgrading to the 2.0.5 client on the Mac results in a connection entry, but all commands fail.

**Output of `podman version`:**

Mac
```
podman version 2.1.1
```

CentOS Stream
```
podman version 2.0.5
```


**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

Mac
```
Error: Get "http://d/v2.0.0/libpod../../../_ping": dial unix ///var/folders/br/6gbf6hbn5pv7bswz5n86zkm40000gn/T/run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes - checked troubleshooting guide

No - have not tested a later version that is packaged for CentOS Stream 


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

2 separate physical boxes on a local wired network.  SSH works.

comments.body: centos8 could update to 2.1.1, and try again

@zhangguanzhang I don't believe that a newer version is packaged.  I find it odd that if there was a break in minor releases that wouldn't be in release notes somewhere.  I am hesitant to build my own package until we know that this wasn't intentional.

I've updated the title, as it was wrong.

This was the other error message I've received

```
% podman system connection add srv 192.168.0.227
Error: failed to connect to  "ssh://bexelbie@192.168.0.227:22": failed to parse 'podman info' results: json: cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field Version.version.APIVersion of type string
```

@jwhonce @baude PTAL
I have a feeling this was unexpected but intentional.   I am not sure we are being backwords compatible with the client, yet.

To add to my confusion, there are three module streams available in CentOS Stream 8:

1.0 which contains the 1.0 binary
2.0 which contains the 1.6.4 binary
rhel8 which contains the 2.0.5 binary

It'd be great to see a stream that was running to provide "latest", which is what I naively assumed 2.0 would be.  Perhaps we should update the docs to suggest when to use the other streams (rhel8 is default) or to state that they are being deprecated.

@rhatdan Thank you
Only podman 2.* support APIV2, so we at least need that version on the client and server.
You need to talk to @fatherlinux about the streams names.

The rhel8 stream is the latest - the others are long-term support streams that will stay on an older Podman version for stability, with critical bugfixes and security updates. They are not deprecated, though I believe the 1.0 stream is approaching that point (I believe the update guarantee is for 2 years, so 1.0 will be deprecated in 8.4 and a new 3.0 stream will be created; but I'm less clear on these points).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't think this is an issue any longer after @mheon explanation, closing.
